title: ABC谋杀案
summary:
After the discovery of an ABC booklet open to the Bexhill train page near the murder victim, Blackstone and Poirot travel toward Bexhill by police car. They are joined by Inspector Croft, a young and somewhat arrogant detective who recently solved the Mabel Home murder. During the journey, Croft discusses the psychology of serial murders and his use of linguistic traps. Poirot learns from Croft that the victim, a twenty-three-year-old waitress from the Turmeric Cat café, was strangled with her own thick woven belt. While Blackstone suggests the use of the belt indicates an animalistic nature, Poirot remains reserved.

Upon arriving in Bexhill, Poirot and Blackstone meet Chief Inspector Carter and Inspector Kelsey. Carter reports that the victim's parents have been notified and that she has an older sister in London. The ABC booklet found at the scene contains no fingerprints and was not purchased locally. The body was discovered at approximately 6:00 a.m. by Colonel Jerome during a morning walk with his dog. The estimated time of death is between midnight and 1:00 a.m. on the twenty-fifth, which aligns with the killer's stated timeline.

Inspectors Croome and Kelsey visit the Turmeric Cat café to investigate. There, they meet the manager, Miss Merion, a thin woman with orange-yellow hair. While Miss Merion expresses distress over the tragedy and its impact on business, she appears excited by the attention. She reveals that the deceased girl was working her second summer at the café but claims to have no further information about her.
